---
title: "test"
author: "alexM"
date: "2026-04-28"
output: html_document
---

# loading and connecting database
```{r}
# Load libraries and database
library(DBI)
library(RSQLite)
library(tidyverse)
 
db_path <- "C:/Users/Sanik/AppData/Local/activitywatch/activitywatch/aw-server/peewee-sqlite.v2.db"
con <- dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), dbname = db_path) # connect to db
# the connect object must be passed to all other functions to do with the db
```

# exploring the database
```{r}
print(dbListTables(con)) # list all contained tables
print(dbListFields(con,"eventmodel")) # list the fields, "columns", in a given table
df = dbReadTable(con,"eventmodel") # read entire table
apps = dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT * FROM eventmodel WHERE bucket_id = 1") # this is an SQL query
websites = dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT timestamp, duration, datastr FROM eventmodel WHERE bucket_id = 3")
dbDisconnect(con)
```
# cleaning and saving rds
```{r}
#lets theoretically clean the data:
# Convert timestamp to datetime
websites$timestamp <- as.POSIXct(websites$timestamp, format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
# Convert duration to numeric if needed
websites$duration <- as.numeric(websites$duration)
#something like that
#now we can save the cleaned data as an rds:
saveRDS(websites, "data/websites_test.rds")
# and we can read that like this:
# df = readRDS("data/websites_test.rds")
```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
apps = readRDS("data/apps_clean.rds")
websites = readRDS("data/websites_clean.rds")
#lets separate the column event_start:

apps <- apps %>% 
  mutate(week=floor_date(event_start, unit="week"),
         day = floor_date(event_start, unit="day"),
         month = floor_date(event_start, unit="month")) %>% 
  mutate(category = case_when(is.na(category) ~ "Other", 
                              category == "browser" ~ "Browser",
                                TRUE ~ category))

websites <- websites %>% 
  mutate(week=floor_date(event_start, unit="week"),
         day = floor_date(event_start, unit="day"),
         month = floor_date(event_start, unit="month")) %>% 
  mutate(category = case_when(is.na(category) ~ "Other", TRUE ~ category))

total_time_apps = apps %>% 
  group_by(week, category) %>%
  summarise(total_time=sum(active_duration))

#now a stacked colourful barchart for the categories:
total_time_apps %>% 
  #filter(category=="Browser") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x= week, y= total_time/3600, fill=category)) +
  theme_minimal()+
  geom_bar(stat="identity")+
  labs(y="Total time (hours)", x="Week")

```
```{r}
total_time_apps %>% group_by(category) %>% 
  summarise(sum(total_time))
```
